commit.message:
feat: implement MatMulNBits operator (com.microsoft contrib op) (#668)

repository.name: emx-onnx-cgen
repository.description: Deterministic ONNX-to-C compiler for embedded and safety-critical systems, generating static, auditable C code with no dynamic memory or runtime dependencies.

commit.message:
feat: trigger website rebuild when registry index changes

Uses peter-evans/repository-dispatch to fire a registry-updated
event on popsicle-registry-website whenever index files or config
are pushed to main.

Requires WEBSITE_DISPATCH_TOKEN secret (PAT with repo scope on
popsicle-registry-website) to be set in this repo's settings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve all security vulnerabilities via pnpm overrides

- Pin brace-expansion@<1.1.13 to exact 1.1.13 (not >=1.1.13 which resolved to 5.x)
- Pin brace-expansion@>=2.0.0 <2.0.3 to 2.0.3
- Pin picomatch@>=4.0.0 <4.0.4 to 4.0.4
- Add path-based overrides: anymatch>picomatch, micromatch>picomatch, readdirp>picomatch
- Pin yaml@>=2.0.0 <2.8.3 to 2.8.3 (Stack Overflow fix)
- Pin flatted, node-forge to exact safe versions
Result: 0 vulnerabilities (was 13 high/moderate)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve risk score calculation for identity-based risk factors

- Lower digital footprint threshold (2+ platforms vs 10+)
- Add scaled risk weights based on platform count
- Add public profile information exposure risk
- Add username correlation risk detection
- Lower cluster confidence threshold (0.7 vs 0.8)
- Add potential linkage detection for any clusters

These changes ensure risk scores reflect OSINT findings even without
file exposures, which is the primary use case for defense.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: redesign editor forms with flat layout and StrataIconPicker

Redesign all four editor views (Agents, Skills, Projects, MCP Servers)
from the old two-column layout with bare TextBox and small-caps labels
to a clean, flat single-column form layout that fits within the
available viewport without overflow.

Changes:
- Replace card-based sections with flat form fields and Strata separator
  dividers between logical groups
- Add StrataIconPicker in a Flyout for emoji selection (Agents, Skills)
  replacing the cramped 72px TextBox
- Use Margin on content StackPanel instead of ScrollViewer Padding to
  prevent horizontal overflow of TextBox controls
- Tighten spacing to ensure all form sections (including Skills, MCP
  Servers, Tools) are visible without scrolling on standard window sizes
- Use localized strings for all field labels
- Keep StrataSetting/StrataSettingGroup only for Type/Enabled toggles in
  MCP Servers where the horizontal label+control layout is appropriate
- Fix StrataIconPicker.SelectIcon to use SetCurrentValue instead of
  SetValue to preserve TwoWay bindings (Strata submodule)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Lumi
repository.description: A personal agentic desktop assistant powered by GitHub Copilot SDK and Avalonia UI

commit.message:
fix: address review comments

Move _validate_since_until() before _print_version_header() in both
summary() and cost() commands so that reversed --since/--until ranges
produce clean Click UsageError output without the version header.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix window close hanging on some PCs

Move disk I/O out of OnClosing so the window disappears instantly.
Window bounds are captured in-memory during OnClosing, then persisted
in ShutdownRequested via Task.Run (avoids UI-thread deadlock when
_writeLock is held by an in-flight save). Both SaveAsync and
CopilotService.DisposeAsync now have timeouts to prevent infinite hangs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Lumi
repository.description: A personal agentic desktop assistant powered by GitHub Copilot SDK and Avalonia UI

commit.message:
fix: address review comments

- Extract _validate_since_until helper to deduplicate reversed-range
  validation between summary and cost commands
- Use isoformat() instead of .date() in UsageError messages to preserve
  time component
- Fix test docstrings to match actual assertions (non-zero vs code 2)
- Update section headers and class names in test_report.py to reflect
  current behavior (no warning emitted for reversed ranges)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add browser and stop guidance to syu app (#144)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 12 platform-specific IPC examples (#64)

* Add file-watcher example with inotify-backed server and tests

Linux directory watcher using inotify that demonstrates IPC notifications.
Server watches directories and broadcasts FileChanged events to clients.
Seven integration tests cover watch/unwatch lifecycle, event notifications
for file creation/modification/deletion, and error handling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add sysmon example with /proc-based CPU, memory, and load monitoring

Server reads /proc/stat, /proc/meminfo, and /proc/loadavg to implement
GetCpuUsage, GetMemoryInfo, and GetLoadAverage RPCs. A monitoring thread
checks thresholds every second and fires HighCpuAlert/LowMemoryAlert
notifications.

Client queries all three methods in a formatted table and listens for
threshold notifications. Test suite covers all RPCs, both notification
types, invalid method handling, and RunLoop dispatch mode (8 tests).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add disk-usage example with simulated volumes

Implement the disk-usage example under examples/macos/disk-usage/:

- disk_device.cpp: Simulated server with 3 volumes (/, /home, /tmp).
  Usage on / grows 1 GB every 3s; fires DiskSpaceLow when > 90%.
- disk_client.cpp: Interactive CLI with list, check <mount>, quit
  commands and bar-chart display. Prints notification alerts.
- disk_test.cpp: 8 Google Test cases covering GetVolumeCount,
  GetVolumeAt (valid/invalid), GetDiskUsage (known/unknown mount),
  DiskSpaceLow notification, UsedPercent consistency, RunLoop dispatch.
- CMakeLists.txt: Builds device, client, and test executables.
- README.md: IDL overview, build/run instructions, sample output.

All volumes are simulated — no platform-specific APIs — so the example
compiles and runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add audio-dsp example: simulated DSP effects processor

Implement server (dsp_device), client CLI (dsp_dashboard), and 12 Google
Test cases covering slot management, parameter updates, effect counting,
signal levels, invalid-slot errors, and ClipDetected/LevelUpdate
notifications.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add can-bus-ecu example: simulated ECU with OBD-II PIDs

Implement ecu_device (server with state machine simulation),
ecu_dashboard (interactive CLI client), and ecu_test (11 Google
Test cases covering all RPC methods, notifications, and RunLoop
dispatch). Cross-platform — compiles on Linux and Windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add BLE Heart Rate Monitor peripheral example

Implement simulated BLE HRM with GATT service model:
- ble_device: server with Heart Rate (0x180D) and Battery (0x180F)
  services, simulation thread for HR/battery updates
- ble_dashboard: interactive CLI client with service discovery,
  characteristic read, and notification subscription
- ble_test: 9 Google Test cases covering service count, get/read/
  write characteristics, notification enable/disable, error cases

All code is cross-platform (no macOS-specific APIs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add motor-controller example with simulated stepper motor

Implements MotorController IPC service with trapezoidal motion profiles,
limit switch detection, stall monitoring, and homing sequence. Includes:

- motor_device: simulated stepper motor server (10ms tick loop)
- motor_dashboard: interactive CLI client with status display
- motor_test: 10 Google Test cases covering all methods and notifications

All code is cross-platform (no Windows-specific APIs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CMake wiring and build warnings for platform examples

- Consolidate platform subdirectory includes in examples/CMakeLists.txt
- Guard Linux-only examples (sysmon, file-watcher) inside linux/CMakeLists.txt
- Remove duplicate add_subdirectory entries from parallel agent commits
- Fix -Werror=unused-result warnings in filewatcher_test.cpp

* Address code review findings across platform examples

- Fix dead code and wrong formula in battery_device timeToEmpty calc
- Move notifyXxx calls outside mutex locks in motor, gpio, procmon, svcstat
- Add can-bus-ecu to windows/CMakeLists.txt (was missing)
- Replace fragile reference capture in ble_device with member m_simRunning
- Add explicit null termination after strncpy in disk_device
- Move ecu_device printf inside lock scope to avoid data race

* Fix motor simulation crossover on coarse-timer platforms

On Windows (~15ms timer resolution) and macOS, a single simulation
tick can overshoot the MoveTo target position. Add post-update
crossover detection in both the production device and test device
to snap to target when position crosses or reaches the destination.

Fixes CI failures: MoveTo_ReachesTarget, MoveWithoutHome_Allowed

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(cli): raise click.UsageError for reversed --since/--until range (#454)

Move reversed date-range validation from _filter_sessions (report layer)
to the summary and cost CLI commands. This replaces the raw Python
UserWarning with a proper click.UsageError that exits with code 2 and
a human-readable message.

- cli.py: Add since > until check in summary() and cost() before
  calling render functions
- report.py: Remove warnings.warn from _filter_sessions; silently
  return [] for reversed ranges
- test_cli.py: Add CliRunner tests for both summary and cost with
  reversed range
- test_report.py: Update existing tests to assert no warning is emitted
- test_e2e.py: Update e2e test to expect non-zero exit instead of
  UserWarning

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: organize Theorems/ into 7 subdirectories

Move 44 theorem files into thematic subdirectories:
- Solvability/ (6): core equation theory (ClosedForm, Invertibility, etc.)
- Clamping/ (5): value clamping (ClampLocal, ClosedFormBox, etc.)
- Iteration/ (5): convergence and contraction (JacobiContraction, BinaryAlpha, etc.)
- Approximation/ (8): near-binary, blur fusion, compositing errors
- Grid/ (10): grid-level and canonical builder theorems
- FixedPrecision/ (6): fixed-width arithmetic theorems
- Evaluation/ (4): metrics and gradient semantics

All imports updated. No functional changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address review comments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract Core.JacobiIteration from Theorems.JacobiContraction

Move 12 definitions (jacobiStep, jacobiIterate, jacobiSpectralRadius, etc.)
and 6 @[simp] accessor lemmas to Core.JacobiIteration. Theorems/JacobiContraction
now imports Core.JacobiIteration and re-exports all names for backward compat.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: add value assertions for render_detail helpers (#453)

- Gap 1: _render_active_period — assert counts and format_tokens output;
  test inactive session produces no output
- Gap 2: _render_header — assert model=None and start_time=None show '—'
- Gap 3: _render_aggregate_stats — assert formatted output tokens (5.0K)
  and API duration (1m) in non-resumed path

Strengthen existing test_active_session_shows_active_period and
test_renders_aggregate_stats with inline value checks.

Closes #453

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract Core.ClosedForm, remove stale imports and dead files

Phase 0: Remove stale imports (Theorems.QuantizationBounds from
FixedPrecision/Coefficients, Theorems.ClosedFormMeanResidual from
FixedPrecision/LocalSolver). Delete non-compiling GlobalSystem.lean
and MultigridSpec.lean.

Phase 1-1: Extract closedFormSolution and related definitions from
Theorems/ClosedForm.lean into Core/ClosedForm.lean. Level/Jacobi.lean
now imports Core.ClosedForm instead of Theorems.ClosedForm, resolving
the layering violation. Backward compatibility maintained via export
in Theorems/ClosedForm.lean.

Also adds docs/refactoring-plan.md with full analysis and phased plan.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add a schedule trigger to ci.yml (Mondays 06:00 UTC) so cargo audit
and npm audit run automatically between releases, catching new CVEs
before the next PR. cancel-in-progress is disabled for scheduled runs
so alerts are never silently dropped.

Document the weekly audit cadence in CONTRIBUTING.md so contributors
know they do not need to run manual audits.

Closes #116

  (#section/item-id format). On load, restores state from hash. jumpToItem
  and selectSection update the hash via history.replaceState so items are
  directly shareable via URL.

- Keyboard navigation in search: ArrowDown/Up moves through results,
  Enter selects focused or sole result, Escape clears query. Focused
  result highlighted with ring-2 ring-sky-400. Added role=combobox,
  aria-expanded, aria-controls ARIA attributes.

- Item chip label: 'Items in this document' label added above pill row.
  Each pill button now has title attribute showing the full item title.

- Favicon: add app/public/favicon.svg and link it in index.html.

Closes #122

refactor: split build_session_summary into focused helpers (#224)

Extract the ~200-line build_session_summary function into 4 focused
private helpers:

- _first_pass(): single pass over events to extract identity, shutdown
  data, and counters
- _detect_resume(): scan post-shutdown events for resume indicators
- _build_completed_summary(): merge shutdown cycles into SessionSummary
- _build_active_summary(): model inference + config fallback for active
  sessions

Two frozen dataclasses (_FirstPassResult, _ResumeInfo) carry state
between phases. The public API (signature + return type) is unchanged.

Closes #224

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: replace O(n) IIFE turn search in Timeline with memoized binary search

The current-turn lookup was an inline IIFE that ran on every render, doing a
linear scan through all turns. During playback currentTime updates at animation
speed (~60fps), so this ran hundreds of times per second on large sessions.

Fix: extract into useMemo keyed on [currentTime, turns]. Since turns are
time-ordered and non-overlapping, use binary search (O(log n)) instead of
linear scan. Falls back to last turn when currentTime is past all turn ranges.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: remove autonomy grants section from public-facing instructions

Auto-push to main, auto-delete files, and auto-run commands are appropriate
for a trusted solo dev context but not safe defaults for a public repo where
contributors' AI agents may read these instructions.

fix: resolve CI failure — add test for negative shutdown_model_calls guard

The diff-cover check failed because line 597 of report.py (the
defensive clamp of shutdown_model_calls to 0 when active_model_calls
exceeds model_calls) was not covered by tests.

Added test_negative_shutdown_model_calls_clamped_to_zero to cover
this edge case, bringing diff coverage to 100%.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: tighten style guide -- font.ui scope, icon registry rule, toolbar overflow pattern

- Narrowed font.ui rule: reserved for BrandWordmark and nav tab buttons only.
  All other UI text (buttons, dropdowns, metrics, coach panel) must use font.mono.
  This matches the DebriefView audit that found 9 violations from the old loose rule.
- Added Icon registry rule: both lucide-react import AND ICON_MAP entry are required.
  Silent null renders caused the missing clock/arrow-right icons in Recent Sessions.
  Dev-mode console.warn is now documented.
- Added toolbar overflow rule: overflow:hidden must not be used on containers
  with absolutely-positioned dropdowns. Documents the AppHeader fix pattern with
  position:relative + z.active on container, z.tooltip on dropdown.
- Updated Review Checklist to match all three new rules.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: show shutdown-only model calls on per-model cost rows (#409)

In render_cost_view, the per-model row displayed s.model_calls (total
across all periods) while all other columns used shutdown-only data from
s.model_metrics. For resumed sessions this caused the visual sum of
per-model + ↳ rows to exceed the grand total.

Subtract s.active_model_calls so the per-model row is consistent with
the other shutdown-only columns. For non-resumed sessions
active_model_calls is 0, so behaviour is unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Track already-scraped usernames in enrichment engine to avoid duplicate API calls
- Skip Twitter/Reddit scraping if username was already processed in Tier 1

- Include risk factors from risk_assessment in report

feat: enable CHANGELOG.md generation via release-please (#108)

* feat: enable CHANGELOG.md generation via release-please (#99)

Remove skip-changelog: true from release-please-config.json so that
release-please generates and maintains CHANGELOG.md automatically from
conventional commits on each release.

Add a 'Changelog' subsection to CONTRIBUTING.md explaining the
conventional-commit prefix-to-section mapping so contributors know
which prefixes appear in the changelog.

Update repository_quality test to assert changelog is not skipped.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add key concepts terminology to configuration guide (#106)

* docs: add key concepts terminology section to configuration guide (#101)

Add a 'Key concepts' section near the top of configuration.md defining
the terms used by the validation flags:
- orphaned item: spec item with no links to adjacent layers
- reciprocal link: two-way bidirectional confirmation of a relationship
- symbol trace: declaration naming the source functions that implement/test an item
- symbol trace coverage: enforcement that all public symbols are claimed

fix: polish UI consistency across Coach, Tracks, and header

- DebriefView: replace all font.ui with font.mono for consistency
- DebriefView: reduce title from fontSize.xl to fontSize.md
- DebriefView: reduce metric values from fontSize.lg to fontSize.md

rustfmt requires the 3-arg assert! macro call to be split across

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: update style guide text token values to match contrast improvements

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: improve contrast and readability without changing layout density

- Bump text token hierarchy in theme.js (primary, secondary, muted, dim, ghost)
- Fix AppHeader: filename uses text.secondary, event count uses text.muted
- Fix AppHeader: remove overflow:hidden that clipped dropdowns; add position+zIndex
- Fix AppHeader: filter dropdown uses font.mono and tighter sizing
- Fix AppHeader: search input uses fontSize.sm, underline-only styling
- Timeline: raise marker min-opacity to Math.max(0.5, intensity*0.8)

No font size, spacing, or layout changes -- density preserved.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: run Playwright E2E tests in browser-app CI job (#97) (#113)

Add steps to the browser-app CI job to install Chromium via Playwright
and execute 'npm run test:e2e' so browser regressions are caught on
every PR.

The webServer in playwright.config.ts starts 'cargo run -- app .' using
the debug build already compiled in the same job, so no extra build step
is needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add jump-to-item button in validation issue detail in syu app (#79) (#112)

When a validation issue's subject is a known spec item ID, show a
'→ View <ID>' button in the IssueDetail panel that calls jumpToItem()
to navigate directly to that spec item.

This creates a tight feedback loop: see a validation problem → click →
inspect the problematic spec item → fix it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove AdminLayout wrapping from all admin page files

AdminLayout is now used as a layout route in App.js via the Outlet
pattern, so individual pages no longer need to wrap their content
with <AdminLayout>...</AdminLayout>.

Changes across all 20 admin page files:
- Removed AdminLayout import
- Replaced <AdminLayout> wrapper with React fragments (<>...</>)
- Updated inline loading/error returns to remove AdminLayout wrapper

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: show planned trace placeholder in syu app when item has no traces (#95) (#111)

When a planned requirement has no tests or a planned feature has no
implementations, render a soft placeholder card explaining what YAML to
add and how to promote the item to 'implemented'.

The placeholder only appears for status: planned items; implemented
items with no traces already surface a validation error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add trailing newline to dist assets; exclude app/dist from end-of-file-fixer (#110)

* fix: add trailing newline to dist assets and exclude app/dist from end-of-file-fixer

The compiled browser-app dist assets were missing end-of-file newlines,
causing the pre-commit end-of-file-fixer to fail on every CI run.

Fix the two affected files and add an exclude pattern so future builds
of app/dist do not trigger the hook.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct App.tsx formatting to satisfy biome check

A line in the search results panel exceeded the max line length,
causing 'npm run check' to fail. Reformatted with vp check --fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: show next-step guidance after syu validate success (#104)

* feat: show next-step guidance after syu validate success (#94)

When 'syu validate' passes with 0 issues, append a 'What to do next'
block listing syu app, syu browse, syu report, and syu show so first-
time users know how to explore the validated workspace.

The block is omitted when there are validation errors (clear action:
fix the errors) and in --format json mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: assert next-step guidance block in validate success output (#94)

Extend check_command_accepts_passing_workspace to verify that
'What to do next:' and 'syu app .' appear in the success output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
